company.name: NVIDIA (Israel)
company.notes: Massive footprint after Mellanox + Run.ai acquisitions. Active AI hiring.

research.status_summary: Aggressively hiring and physically expanding across Israel — ~6,000 employees as of mid-2026 with 350–400 open Israel roles live on LinkedIn/Glassdoor, zero signals of layoffs or freeze.
research.hiring_policy: NVIDIA Israel hires directly via its global Workday ATS (nvidia.wd5.myworkdayjobs.com); roles are on-site or hybrid at Yokneam, Tel Aviv (18 floors, Rubinstein Twin Towers), Ra'anana, Be'er Sheva, and Jerusalem — no EOR-friendly or fully-remote posture publicly stated for Israel headcount, and Israeli citizenship is not a listed hard requirement per korotchaim.com (last verified 2026-05-02).
recent_news: Networking division (largely built in Israel) hit ~$15B in quarterly revenue — almost tripling YoY — as of NVIDIA's latest earnings (May 2026), cementing Israel as the company's single largest revenue-generating R&D hub outside the US.

NVIDIA formally confirmed its Kiryat Tivon mega-campus (160,000 sqm, 10,000-employee capacity, multibillion-shekel investment, construction 2027–2031) — the first international tech campus in Israel built on owned, not leased, land.

NVIDIA acquired Israeli AI startup Illumex (~$60M, February 2026), folding its enterprise knowledge-graph / Generative Semantic Fabric team into NVIDIA Israel — the third Israeli AI acquisition in two years (after Deci and Run:ai).

Be'er Sheva R&D center tripled to 3,000 sqm at Gav-Yam Negev Tech Park, targeting 'hundreds' of new hires including chip developers, HW/SW engineers, and university graduates — move completed H1 2026.

Jensen Huang publicly named Israel NVIDIA's 'second home' at CES 2026, and CFO confirmed Mellanox-derived networking revenue now exceeds $31B annually — more than 4× the original $7B acquisition price.
research.growth_signal: Headcount has grown from ~2,000 (at Mellanox acquisition) to ~6,000 today and is on a stated trajectory toward 10,000+ when the Kiryat Tivon campus opens (~2031), making NVIDIA the fastest-scaling large tech employer in Israel and likely its largest private employer within a decade.
research.fit_for_lior: NVIDIA Israel is a strong match for Lior across all three threads: the Run:ai and Deci acquisitions seeded a dedicated AI-software / LLM product org in Tel Aviv (Creative AI + Product Analyst fit), the 'Manager, AI Agents and Applied Research' and 'Software Solutions Architect – NVIS' roles listed in TLV signal appetite for product-adjacent AI builders, and NVIDIA's Inception Program (1,000+ Israeli startups) offers a Vibe Coder / developer-relations entry point for someone bridging creative AI and technical product work.
